channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: 'A remarkable achievement': Neanderthals may have practised dentistry
item.description: Archaeologists have found a hole in a 59,000-year-old tooth, which they say was drilled to treat a painful cavity. The find suggests Neanderthals could perform complex medical procedures.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Phiddian

media:group.media:description: A Neanderthal had a tooth drilled to treat a painful cavity some 59,000 years ago, according to a new study.

item.title: Kate tours Italy in first overseas visit since cancer recovery
item.description: The royal is visiting Reggio Emilia, the Italian city famed for its approach to early childhood education.

media:group.media:description: The Princess of Wales is visiting the Italian city of Reggio Emilia, which is notable for its innovative approach to early childhood education.

item.title: Six dead as Russia launches over 800 drones at Ukraine
item.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy accuses Russia of attempting to overload Ukraine's defences in an hours-long onslaught.

media:group.media:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says six people are dead after one of Russia's biggest drone attacks of the war so far.

item.title: At least 12 people killed in Israeli strikes on cars in Lebanon
item.description: The deadly drone strikes come on the eve of a new round of peace talks between Israel and Lebanon in the United States.

media:group.media:description: An Israeli airstrike hit a car in the Lebanese coastal town of Jiyeh, south of Beirut.

item.title: UK MP Nigel Farage facing inquiry over $9m gift from crypto billionaire
item.description: Britain's parliamentary watchdog will begin an investigation into whether Reform UK leader Nigel Farage, breached its rules by accepting a 5 million pound ($9.31 million) gift which he did not declare.

media:group.media:description: Britain's Reform UK office is communicating with the parliamentary commissioner for standards.

item.title: Donald Trump arrives in Beijing ahead of Xi Jinping summit
item.description: The ​US and China are expected to agree to forums to facilitate mutual trade and investment.

media:group.media:description: US President Donald Trump has arrived in Beijing.

item.title: Shots fired in Philippine senate as ICC seeks to arrest ex-police chief
item.description: Authorities are still trying to work out who fired the gun shots inside the senate building in the Philippines, prompting people to run for cover.
item.dc:creator: Claire Campbell

media:group.media:description: Military personnel enter the senate building where Philippine Senator Ronald dela Rosa has been under protective custody since Monday.

item.title: Australian government secures aircraft to fly home Hantavirus cruise passengers
item.description: A suitable aircraft to bring home passengers from the Hantavirus-hit cruise has been secured and will repatriate the group of six later this week.
item.dc:creator: Anna Cox

media:group.media:description: Mark Butler earlier revealed the struggle to secure a suitable aircraft for the mission.

item.title: Graphic novel wins $60k Stella Prize for first time in award's 14-year history
item.description: Lee Lai's debut graphic novel, Stone Fruit, was longlisted for the Stella Prize in 2022. Four years on, she's won the prestigious award for her latest book, Cannon.
item.dc:creator: Nicola Heath

media:group.media:description: Lee Lai, an Australian cartoonist based in Montreal, has won the 2026 Stella Prize for her graphic novel Cannon.

item.title: Is the 2026 budget a missed opportunity for First Nations people?
item.description: First Nations experts have their say on whether the $1.2 billion for Closing the Gap initiatives announced in this year's budget is likely to hit the mark.
item.dc:creator: Tahnee Jash and Stephanie Boltje

media:group.media:description: Indigenous people have double the rate of unmet housing needs.

item.title: NT government accused of 'politicising tragedy' with child protection laws
item.description: Peak Aboriginal bodies have accused the Northern Territory government of using Kumanjayi Little Baby's alleged murder as political cover to push ahead with sweeping changes to child protection laws.
item.dc:creator: Jacqueline Breen

media:group.media:description: Ben Grimes says the "rushed" law changes represent "the politicisation of a tragedy".

item.title: Magic Round to remain in Brisbane until 2032 after government reaches deal with NRL
item.description: Queensland's deal with the NRL to host Magic Round was originally due to end after 2027, but the state government has reached a deal with the NRL to extend until 2032.
item.dc:creator: Ned Hammond

media:group.media:description: The state government has revealed the NRL's Magic Round will remain in Brisbane until at least 2032.

item.title: Maroons turn to 'unbelievable' Upton in bid to correct errors and discipline
item.description: Queensland needs a win to keep its State of Origin hopes alive and is turning to the inspirational Tamika Upton to provide it.

media:group.media:description: The Maroons are banking on Tamika Upton (centre) inspiring them to a Game 2 win to keep the Origin series alive.

item.title: Protesters demand 'stop the chop' at rally opposing North Adelaide golf project
item.description: More than 2,000 people gathered at Parliament House in Adelaide to protest against the chopping down of trees to make way for the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment.
item.dc:creator: Briana Fiore and Daniel Keane

media:group.media:description: Hundreds of protesters gathered at Parliament House at a rally that spilled onto the street.

item.title: NSW premier attacks 'kangaroo court' after report into $37m grants scheme
item.description: Chris Minns responds to claims from a parliamentary inquiry that the government "unlawfully redirected" millions of dollars of public money and accused witnesses of lying under oath.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Lewis

media:group.media:description: NSW Premier Chris Minns took aim at the head of the committee, Greens MLC Abigail Boyd.

item.title: Notorious paedophile's lawyer says indefinite jail would be an 'abuse of process'
item.description: A lawyer for paedophile former magistrate Peter Liddy asks a court to permanently stay an application to keep his client behind bars, but a lawyer for the SA Attorney-General says Liddy continues to deny his crimes.
item.dc:creator: Jordanna Schriever

media:group.media:description: Peter Liddy was jailed in 2001 for 25 years for the sexual abuse of children between 1983 and 1986.

item.title: Invictus Australia budget cut labelled potentially 'life-threatening'
item.description: The federal government is urged to reverse its decision to cut funding to Invictus Australia, an organisation that supports veterans.
item.dc:creator: David Mark

media:group.media:description: Veterans are concerned they will continue to struggle mentally without the assistance of Invictus Australia after the latest budget cut.

item.title: Labor upgrades migration numbers by 55,000 in federal budget
item.description: The federal budget reveals the government expects more migrants to arrive to Australia than previously thought, even as it faces a campaign from the Coalition and One Nation to slash migration rates.
item.dc:creator: Jake Evans

media:group.media:description: International students are the most common temporary migrants arriving in Australia.

item.title: Why Ivan Cleary's decision to leave Penrith is as unprecedented as his success
item.description: Ivan Cleary is doing what no other coach of his stature has ever done in Australian rugby league history, walking away from the NRL with his team at the height of their powers.
item.dc:creator: Nick Campton

media:group.media:description: Cleary will coach Penrith until the end of next season.

item.title: US author to be sentenced for killing husband then writing a book on grief
item.description: Kouri Richins was convicted of aggravated murder after poisoning her husband with fentanyl and self-publishing a children's book about coping with grief.

media:group.media:description: Kouri Richins is set to be sentenced for her husband's murder after writing a children's book about grief.

item.title: Miner wins right to sue council for $22m in years-long road dispute
item.description: A legal stoush between a Central Queensland council and one of Australia's largest coal mines deepens in Queensland's Supreme Court.
item.dc:creator: Liam O'Connell

media:group.media:description: David Luke's house at Mooray Station is close to the road used by heavy vehicles to access the mine.

item.title: John Travolta's luxury jet bound for aviation museum arrives by ship
item.description: After nearly a decade of delays and millions of dollars in repairs, John Travolta's old luxury jet needed a boat to get to Australia.
item.dc:creator: Justin Huntsdale

media:group.media:description: John Travolta's old luxury jet arrives at Port Kembla after two months at sea.

item.title: Whyalla steelworks remains 'fiscal risk' in budget as SA waits for freight bypass funding
item.description: The federal government's ongoing financial support for the Whyalla steelworks remains a "fiscal risk" to the budget. Meanwhile, the South Australian government continues to hold out for more Commonwealth funding for a major road project.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Kelsall and Arj Ganesan

media:group.media:description: A view from near the blast furnace of the Whyalla steelworks in March 2025.

item.title: Woman stole parrot from pet shop by hiding it under top, police allege
item.description: South Australian police swoop on the alleged perpetrator of a parrot theft at a suburban Adelaide pet shop, but say the bird has not been found.

media:group.media:description: SA Police say the bird, a turquoise green-cheeked conure, was from a Melrose Park pet store.

item.title: $150m compensation payout for Yindjibarndi slammed as too little
item.description: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to hand over the largest native title compensation payout in Australian history. But some say it falls painfully short of what is owed.
item.dc:creator: Alistair Bates and Mietta Adams

media:group.media:description: Michael Woodley speaks after Yindjibarndi was awarded $150.1 million in compensation from Andrew Forrest's Fortescue.
